name: Investment Due Diligence — Series B SaaS
description: Due diligence on a Series B SaaS company. The projector cross-checks the investment thesis from multiple structural angles, revealing contradictions the pitch deck hides.
direction: Determine whether the company's growth trajectory is structurally sustainable or dependent on non-repeatable factors

tensions.claim: ARR grew 3x last year but 40% came from 3 enterprise deals — concentration risk disguised as hypergrowth
Net revenue retention is 130% but driven by price increases not usage expansion — customers pay more for the same thing
Gross margin is 72% but includes significant professional services revenue booked as subscription
CAC payback is 14 months which looks healthy but channel costs are rising 20% quarter over quarter
Engineering team is strong but 3 of 5 senior engineers joined in the last 6 months — untested under pressure
Market is shifting toward their category but timing advantage lasts 18 months before incumbents respond
Product has unique workflow engine but the moat is in the data network effect which requires 500+ customers to activate
Founders have deep domain expertise but neither has scaled a company past 100 employees before
Two well-funded competitors exist but are targeting different segments — the segments may converge at scale
Burn multiple is 1.8x which is efficient but leaves no budget for the sales team expansion the plan requires
Largest customer is 15% of revenue and their contract renews in 4 months — losing them resets the growth narrative
New regulations make their compliance features mandatory but regulatory timelines are unpredictable and often delayed
Core ML model is proprietary but built on open-source foundations that improve weekly — the proprietary advantage may erode
Asking 25x ARR based on growth rate but comparable exits are at 12-15x — premium requires proving durability of growth
